name: Verdict Shape Canonicalization (review follow-up)

files_modify.purpose: In Section 4 ('The JSON Verdict Parse Rule'), the fenced ```text gate-rule block (currently at approximately lines 73-78) reads: 'final_verdict.approved === true' / 'AND final_verdict.issues.length === 0' / 'AND final_verdict.missing_acceptance_coverage.length === 0' / 'AND final_verdict.unsupported_claims.length === 0'. Drop the 'final_verdict.' prefix from ONLY the three AND lines (issues / missing_acceptance_coverage / unsupported_claims); the 'final_verdict.approved === true' line is unchanged. The existing ```json example immediately below (approximately lines 82-84) -- {"final_verdict": {"approved": true}, "issues": [], "missing_acceptance_coverage": [], "unsupported_claims": []} -- already matches the corrected sibling-array shape and is NOT touched. This edit supersedes the verbatim-fragment anchor asserted by contract 009's verification[5] (which required the prefixed forms 'final_verdict.issues.length === 0' etc. to survive byte-for-byte); those prefixed fragments are retired by this contract, and the corrected unprefixed fragments become the new verbatim anchor for any future contract. No other body text changes.

In Section 4 ('The JSON Verdict Parse Rule'), the fenced ```text gate-rule block (currently at approximately lines 96-101) duplicates AGF.md's prefixed predicate exactly. Apply the identical fix: drop the 'final_verdict.' prefix from the three AND lines (issues / missing_acceptance_coverage / unsupported_claims), leaving 'final_verdict.approved === true' unchanged, so that after this edit the block is byte-identical to the corrected standard/AGF.md block -- this string-consistency between the two files is an existing QA cross-consistency anchor (per the 009 history entry establishing 01_execution.md as a 'restated, not redefined' reference to AGF.md Section 4). No other body text changes.

In Section 7 ('Verdict JSON Formats'), the fenced ```json example (currently at approximately lines 157-166) nests 'issues', 'missing_acceptance_coverage', and 'unsupported_claims' as keys inside 'final_verdict' alongside 'approved'. Restructure the example so 'final_verdict' contains ONLY 'approved', and 'issues' / 'missing_acceptance_coverage' / 'unsupported_claims' become top-level sibling keys of 'final_verdict' in the same JSON object -- matching the canonical shape used by standard/AGF.md's example and by every verdict the pipeline actually emits per the orchestrator's JSON Verdict Parse Rule. AH-1: re-read the four bullet lines immediately following the block (describing 'approved', 'issues', 'missing_acceptance_coverage', 'unsupported_claims') after restructuring to confirm the 'approved -- true only when every other field below is satisfied' phrasing still reads correctly against the new top-level key ordering; adjust that bullet's wording only if the restructuring makes it misleading, otherwise leave it as-is. The note at the end of Section 7 stating a QA verdict extends this shape per schema/qa_verdict.schema.json is NOT touched. No other body text changes.

Under the existing '## [Unreleased]' heading (which remains topmost, preceding '## [0.1.0]'), add one concise bullet under the '### Fixed' subsection noting the verdict-shape canonicalization: the JSON verdict shape is now consistent across standard/AGF.md, methodology/01_execution.md, and tools/SKILL.md on the sibling-array form (final_verdict holds only 'approved'; 'issues', 'missing_acceptance_coverage', and 'unsupported_claims' are top-level siblings of final_verdict, matching actual pipeline practice). No other CHANGELOG text changes; no reordering of existing entries.

non_goals: No changes to schema/qa_verdict.schema.json -- it validates .agent/qa/NNN.json, a distinct artifact family from the verdict-shape prose/examples this contract corrects
No edits to any completed contract, its approvals, or its evidence/qa records (000-009) -- these are immutable audit records
No files touched beyond the 4 listed in scope.files_modify
No change to standard/AGF.md's existing ```json example block -- it already reflects the corrected sibling-array shape and is left byte-identical
No change to tools/SKILL.md's closing Section 7 note referencing schema/qa_verdict.schema.json's extension of this shape
No change to the 'final_verdict.approved === true' line in either predicate block -- only the three AND lines lose their prefix
No change to any other section of AGF.md, 01_execution.md, or SKILL.md beyond the single fenced block (or, for SKILL.md, the fenced block plus its immediately adjacent bullet prose) identified above

verification.acceptance_test: zero 'final_verdict.issues' / 'final_verdict.missing' / 'final_verdict.unsupported' strings remain in the three touched shipped .md files, count-guarded on file presence
verification.command: files=(standard/AGF.md methodology/01_execution.md tools/SKILL.md); fail=0; for f in "${files[@]}"; do test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; fail=1; continue; }; hit=$(grep -n 'final_verdict\.\(issues\|missing\|unsupported\)' "$f" || true); if [ -n "$hit" ]; then echo "STALE PREFIX in $f:"; echo "$hit"; fail=1; fi; done; test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo 'prefixed form(s) found above'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: standard/AGF.md's Section 4 gate-rule fenced block contains the four corrected conditions verbatim: 'final_verdict.approved === true' unprefixed-AND lines for issues/missing_acceptance_coverage/unsupported_claims
verification.command:
test -f standard/AGF.md || { echo 'MISSING standard/AGF.md'; exit 1; }; python3 -c '
import re
text = open("standard/AGF.md").read()
m = re.search(r"## 4\..*?```text\n(.*?)\n```", text, re.S)
assert m, "gate-rule text-fenced block not found under Section 4"
block = m.group(1)
expected = [
    "final_verdict.approved === true",
    "AND issues.length === 0",
    "AND missing_acceptance_coverage.length === 0",
    "AND unsupported_claims.length === 0",
]
lines = [l.strip() for l in block.strip().splitlines()]
assert lines == expected, f"block mismatch: {lines} != {expected}"
print("PASS")
'

verification.acceptance_test: standard/AGF.md and methodology/01_execution.md Section 4 gate-rule fenced blocks are byte-identical after the fix (existing QA cross-consistency anchor)
verification.command:
test -f standard/AGF.md || { echo 'MISSING standard/AGF.md'; exit 1; }; test -f methodology/01_execution.md || { echo 'MISSING methodology/01_execution.md'; exit 1; }; python3 -c '
import re
def extract(path):
    text = open(path).read()
    m = re.search(r"## 4\..*?```text\n(.*?)\n```", text, re.S)
    assert m, f"gate-rule text-fenced block not found in {path}"
    return m.group(1).strip()
a = extract("standard/AGF.md")
b = extract("methodology/01_execution.md")
assert a == b, f"blocks differ:\nAGF.md:\n{a}\n---\n01_execution.md:\n{b}"
print("PASS")
'

verification.acceptance_test: tools/SKILL.md Section 7's fenced json example parses as valid JSON with 'issues', 'missing_acceptance_coverage', and 'unsupported_claims' as top-level keys (siblings of 'final_verdict'), and 'approved' nested inside 'final_verdict'
verification.command:
test -f tools/SKILL.md || { echo 'MISSING tools/SKILL.md'; exit 1; }; python3 -c '
import re, json
text = open("tools/SKILL.md").read()
m = re.search(r"## 7\..*?```json\n(.*?)\n```", text, re.S)
assert m, "Section 7 json-fenced example not found"
doc = json.loads(m.group(1))
top_level = {"issues", "missing_acceptance_coverage", "unsupported_claims"}
assert top_level <= set(doc.keys()), f"missing top-level key(s): {top_level - set(doc.keys())}"
assert "final_verdict" in doc and isinstance(doc["final_verdict"], dict), "final_verdict missing or not an object"
assert "approved" in doc["final_verdict"], "approved missing from final_verdict"
assert not (top_level & set(doc["final_verdict"].keys())), "arrays still nested inside final_verdict"
print("PASS")
'

verification.acceptance_test: standard/AGF.md's existing json example (immediately after the gate-rule block) still parses and matches the same top-level-sibling shape as tools/SKILL.md's example (consistency across both examples)
verification.command:
test -f standard/AGF.md || { echo 'MISSING standard/AGF.md'; exit 1; }; python3 -c '
import re, json
text = open("standard/AGF.md").read()
m = re.search(r"```text\n.*?\n```\s*\n.*?```json\n(.*?)\n```", text, re.S)
assert m, "json-fenced example following the text-fenced gate-rule block not found"
doc = json.loads(m.group(1))
top_level = {"issues", "missing_acceptance_coverage", "unsupported_claims"}
assert top_level <= set(doc.keys()), f"missing top-level key(s): {top_level - set(doc.keys())}"
assert "final_verdict" in doc and isinstance(doc["final_verdict"], dict), "final_verdict missing or not an object"
assert doc["final_verdict"].get("approved") is True, "final_verdict.approved must be true in this example"
assert not (top_level & set(doc["final_verdict"].keys())), "arrays nested inside final_verdict, shape mismatch vs SKILL.md"
print("PASS")
'

verification.acceptance_test: CHANGELOG.md's '## [Unreleased]' section mentions the verdict-shape canonicalization and remains topmost, preceding '## [0.1.0]'
verification.command: test -f CHANGELOG.md || { echo 'MISSING CHANGELOG.md'; exit 1; }; unreleased_line=$(grep -n '^## \[Unreleased\]' CHANGELOG.md | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); v010_line=$(grep -n '^## \[0.1.0\]' CHANGELOG.md | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); test -n "$unreleased_line" && test -n "$v010_line" && test "$unreleased_line" -lt "$v010_line" || { echo 'Unreleased section missing or does not precede 0.1.0'; exit 1; }; block=$(sed -n "${unreleased_line},${v010_line}p" CHANGELOG.md); echo "$block" | grep -Eqi 'verdict.shape|final_verdict' && echo PASS || { echo 'Unreleased block does not mention the verdict-shape canonicalization'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: regression: the 3 touched .md files (standard/AGF.md, methodology/01_execution.md, tools/SKILL.md) still start with frontmatter containing all 6 required NDS keys
verification.command: files=(standard/AGF.md methodology/01_execution.md tools/SKILL.md); fail=0; for f in "${files[@]}"; do test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; fail=1; continue; }; test "$(head -1 "$f")" = '---' || { echo "NOT FRONTMATTER: $f"; fail=1; }; for k in id title description version status authoritative_source; do grep -q "^$k:" "$f" || { echo "MISSING $k in $f"; fail=1; }; done; done; test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo 'frontmatter regression found above'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: regression: zero untagged code-fence openers remain repo-wide across shipped .md files (not just the 3 touched files)
verification.command: for d in standard methodology registry templates schema tools; do test -d "$d" || { echo "MISSING dir $d"; exit 1; }; done; fail=0; while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do bad=$(awk -v f="$f" 'BEGIN{intxt=0} /^```/{ if (intxt==0) { tag=$0; sub(/^```/,"",tag); if (tag=="") print f":"NR; intxt=1 } else { intxt=0 } }' "$f"); if [ -n "$bad" ]; then echo "$bad"; fail=1; fi; done < <(find CONTEXT.md README.md standard methodology registry templates schema tools -name '*.md' -print0); test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo 'untagged fence(s) found above'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: regression: repo-wide shipped-content NizamIQ-branding sweep remains clean after this contract's edits (guarded so an absent-directory false pass cannot occur)
verification.command: for d in standard methodology registry templates schema tools; do test -d "$d" || { echo "MISSING dir $d, refusing to trust an absence-of-match result"; exit 1; }; done; test -f CONTEXT.md && test -f README.md && test -f CHANGELOG.md && test -f NIZAM.json && test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING one or more root files, refusing to trust an absence-of-match result'; exit 1; }; if grep -ril 'nizamiq' CONTEXT.md README.md CHANGELOG.md NIZAM.json bootstrap.sh standard/ methodology/ registry/ templates/ schema/ tools/ 2>/dev/null; then echo FAIL; exit 1; else echo PASS; fi

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/010-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/010-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/010-verify-09.txt for the ninth). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
